title: Porpax lichenora
intro: Porpax lichenora, also known as Indian pipe, is a rare and unique orchid species that grows in shaded, woodland environments and obtains its nutrients by parasitizing fungi, rather than photosynthesis. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Porpax lichenora include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, with the latter being one of the most common herbal preparations made with Porpax lichenora, often used to ease respiratory distress.
taxonomy: Porpax lichenora is classified as a genus and species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Porpax is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. Further classification includes its order Asparagales and its class Liliopsida. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae.
